perturbation.original_text: 1. APPOINTMENT 1.1. WLI hereby grants to Power2Ship the non-exclusive right and license to distribute certain WLI's products and services (the "Products" or "Units") and software programs ("Licensed Programs") to Power2Ship's customers (which are end users) located in North America. "Products" or "Units" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's G3001 GPSLink mobile terminal as defined in Section 1.2. "Licensed Programs" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's MidLink Middleware software and G3001 programming tools.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. APPOINTMENT 1.1. WLI hereby grants to Power2Ship the non-exclusive right and license to distribute certain WLI's products and services (the "Goods" or "Units") and software programs ("Licensed Programs") to Power2Ship's customers (which are end users) located in North America. "Products" or "Units" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's G3001 GPSLink mobile terminal as defined in Section 1.2. "Licensed Programs" in this Agreement are limited to WLI's MidLink Middleware software and G3001 programming tools.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Products' is changed to 'Goods'. This introduces inconsistency because other sections still use 'Products', leading to confusion whether 'Goods' and 'Products' are interchangeable. In Section 3, for instance, references to 'Product' and 'Licensed Programs' continue to exist, therefore creating a contradiction in definition of 'Goods' and Products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. PRICES 3.1. Subject to Section 3.6 below the price of the Product, excluding the Licensed Programs, is $[*] per Unit. The total price for both of the Licensed Programs together is $[*] per month per Product ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall begin on the date the Product is shipped to a customer and shall be paid by Power2Ship to WLI by the 7th of each month for the prior month (prorated if the Unit was shipped during the prior month) and shall continue for as long as the Product is active on any wireless network. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Power2Ship is obligated to pay the License Fee to WLI for a minimum of 36 months.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Products' as referring to 'WLI's G3001 GPSLink mobile terminal'. The changed text replaces 'Products' with 'Goods'. The contradicted text refers to 'Product' when setting prices. Because the definition of 'Product' is still used in Section 3, and the original definition in Section 1.1 specifically limited 'Products' to 'WLI's G3001 GPSLink mobile terminal,' the change to 'Goods' creates a contradiction. Now, 'Goods' is undefined in Section 3 and it's unclear if pricing is intended for 'Goods', 'Products', or both, because there is no explicit interchangeability defined, triggering a Misaligned Terminology contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. DELIVERY, TITLE AND RISK OF LOSS 6.1. Delivery shall be F.O.B. WLI's facility in Lyndhurst, New Jersey. WLI shall use its reasonable efforts to deliver Products to Power2Ship on the date of delivery specified by Power2Ship. WLI will use its reasonable efforts to notify Power2Ship of any delays in scheduled delivery dates.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. DELIVERY, TITLE AND RISK OF LOSS 6.1. Delivery shall be F.O.B. WLI's facility in Lyndhurst, New Jersey. WLI shall use its reasonable efforts to deliver Items to Power2Ship on the date of delivery specified by Power2Ship. WLI will use its reasonable efforts to notify Power2Ship of any delays in scheduled delivery dates.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Products' is replaced with 'Items'. It's now unclear if 'Items' encompasses everything previously defined as 'Products' or only some subset, particularly in context of warranty claims, since the original term used in sections like 11.1 is 'Products'. It could create uncertainty on what has delivery obligation, as the warranty speaks on products but delivery obligation refers to items.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. WARRANTY AND INDEMNIFICATION 11.1. WLI warrants the Products pursuant to the terms of its hardware limited warranty and limited software warranty set forth in Exhibit B hereto. Subject to the terms and conditions of Exhibit A, WLI agrees, in its sole discretion, to repair or replace at its sole cost and expense, any defective Products or parts thereof which are returned to it within the applicable warranty period, provided that the Products have not been altered or repaired other than with authorization from WLI and by its approved procedures; that the Products have not been subjected to misuse, improper maintenance, negligence or accident; that the Products have not been damaged by excessive physical or electrical stress; and that the Products have not had their serial number or any part thereof altered, defaced or removed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Products' to 'Items' in the delivery clause (6.1) creates a potential contradiction with the warranty clause (11.1), which specifically refers to 'Products'. If 'Items' is interpreted more broadly or narrowly than 'Products,' it introduces ambiguity regarding what is covered under the warranty. This uncertainty is a strong contradiction because it creates confusion on the obligations regarding warranty if delivery obligation refers to a different subset than what's covered under warranty, leading to unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 10. TECHNICAL INFORMATION AND SERVICES 10.1. WLI shall provide, at no cost to Power2Ship, reasonable training, including but not limited to, training of Power2Ship's sales and technical personnel, to enable Power2Ship to meets its obligations under this Agreement, including its obligation to provide service or support to end-users following termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. TECHNICAL INFORMATION AND SERVICES 10.1. WLI shall provide, at no cost to Power2Ship, reasonable training, including but not limited to, training of Power2Ship's sales and technical personnel, to enable Power2Ship to meets its obligations under this Agreement, including its obligation to provide service or assistance to end-users following termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'support' is being replaced by 'assistance'. It causes in-text contradiction, in 10.3 which uses the term 'support' to explicitly discuss the technical support obligations of Power2Ship and WLI. By changing to 'assistance' is 10.1 creates ambiguity regarding the scope of the assistance. Is it only referring to initial setup and usage, or is also encompass the comprehensive technical problem solving that the text describes as 'support'?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.3. Power2Ship will receive the first customer call and will have to maintain a minimum inventory level of spare Units to service its customers within 48 hours. WLI will support an end-user only upon Power2Ship's request and only after Power2Ship has tried to provide the support and has failed to fix the problem. WLI will not be involved in any end-user training.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 10.1 uses the term 'support', which aligns with the term 'support' used in section 10.3. The changed text replaces 'support' with 'assistance' in section 10.1, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text (10.3) explicitly discusses Power2Ship and WLI's technical support obligations. By changing 'support' to 'assistance' in 10.1, the scope of Power2Ship's and WLI's obligations becomes uncertain, especially concerning technical problem solving described as 'support' in 10.3. This creates a direct conflict because it's unclear whether 'assistance' encompasses the same level of technical help as 'support', leading to a strong in-text contradiction, especially since training in 10.1 should enable Power2Ship to provide end-user support.
